title: Applications, Productivity, Remote Access, Scripting, and AI-Assisted Support · Device Forensics
summary: A realistic applications, productivity, remote access, scripting, and ai-assisted support ticket contains one plausible but causally wrong diagnosis or unsafe action. Find the first unsupported inference and repair the evidence chain. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in application support and bounded automation evidence pack and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debug…
objectives: Validate authoritative source, signature, license, architecture, OS, hardware, dependency, disk, network, account, data format, accessibility, compatibility, and rollback before install.
Preserve content, formulas, fonts, links, metadata, permissions, comments, accessibility, automation, print, and changed-client behavior across productivity formats.
Select attended and unattended remote methods with identity, consent, scope, encryption, least privilege, recording, clipboard, file transfer, disconnect, and local fallback controls.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Application Readiness
steps.instruction:
debug case — A team installs apps, remotes into endpoints, runs scripts, and trusts AI suggestions without provenance, consent, secrets, tests, or rollback. The debug variation 4 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Validate authoritative source, signature, license, architecture, OS, hardware, dependency, disk, network, account, data format, accessibility, compatibility, and rollback before install.

Inspect the Application Readiness trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Application Readiness.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for validate authoritative source, signature, license, architecture, os, hardware, dependency, disk, network, account, data format, accessibility, compatibility, and rollback before install. For CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Application Readiness, keep Application Readiness connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Application Readiness, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Application Readiness, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Application Readiness precondition, or invariant that must hold.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Ai Support Boundary, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Ai Support Boundary precondition, or invariant that must hold.

The debug variation 2 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Ai Support Boundary, keep Ai Support Boundary connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use AI for bounded drafting or hypothesis generation while protecting data, validating sources and outputs, resisting injection, preserving human authorization, and documenting non-claims.
